nodes.label: Foundation
nodes.goal: Stand up the Next.js + shadcn + Tailwind + VCS scaffold so all later phases ship UI into a working site.

nodes.label: Home (Brand)
nodes.goal: Story-led home page that mirrors the 2sranch.com aesthetic and makes UPAEC's differentiators legible in the first scroll.

nodes.label: /boxes Catalog
nodes.goal: Render the full box catalog with the 27 product photos and the Order Extra add-on section.

nodes.label: Order Flow
nodes.goal: Wire up Square Customer + Order + Payments end-to-end with confirmation and webhook reconciliation.

nodes.label: Lead Capture / Contact
nodes.goal: Capture pre-launch leads and ongoing inquiries via Square Customer creation; protect forms from bots.

nodes.label: Launch
nodes.goal: Production deploy with custom domain, env management, analytics, and a real sandbox-to-prod smoke before flipping live.

nodes.label: Rebrand display name '7Greens' -> '7G Greens' (with space) across all UI / copy / metadata. Touches: lib/site/constants.
nodes.goal: Rebrand display name '7Greens' -> '7G Greens' (with space) across all UI / copy / metadata. Touches: lib/site/constants.ts (SITE.brand + tagline references), components/chrome/SiteHeader.tsx (logo composition currently splits '7'+'Greens' two-tone — extend to '7G'+'Greens'), components/chrome/SiteFooter.tsx, components/sections/* (any inline mentions), app/layout.tsx (metadata.title default + openGraph.title), app/opengraph-image.tsx (text rendering), app/icon.tsx (still just '7' OK or update to '7G'?), README.md, .planning/PROJECT.md (legalShort + brand strings). DOES NOT change: package.json name (workspace id stays '7greens'), Vercel project slug, GitHub repo (B2Gdevs/7ggreens already encodes 7g-greens), projectid '7greens' in gad config. Pure display-text refactor.

nodes.label: Primary CTA is Order-a-box
nodes.summary: Operator chose live commerce in v1 — Square Orders + Payments scope is in-flight, not just lead-capture. Affects Phase 4 size + Square SDK surface area.

nodes.label: Use existing photos + flag lifestyle gap
nodes.summary: 27 SKU photos exist; documentary lifestyle imagery (2sranch.com style) does not. Don't block launch on photo shoot — ship with placeholders for hero/story slots, source/commission later.

nodes.label: Submodule + monorepo workspace-linked
nodes.summary: Operator wants own repo for 7greens AND monorepo package consumption. Submodule pattern serves both. Future-proof for when gad-visual-context publishes to npm and 7greens can detach.

nodes.label: Cut a new GAD release — version bump, CHANGELOG entry, `bun build --compile` rebuild, git tag, and `gh release create` with artifacts attached. Operator-triggered only, no CI, no npm publish. Trigger this skill whenever the user asks to "cut a release", "ship v<N>", "publish a new release", "bump the version", or any variation that means "the canonical GAD repo should have a new downloadable installer artifact on GitHub Releases." Canonical-repo-only: refuses to run outside the MagicbornStudios/get-anything-done clone. See decisions gad-188 (distribution is GitHub Releases + executable download, no GitHub Actions), gad-278 (bun build --compile is canonical), gad-279 (gad-129 superseded, no public npm).

Source visual assets (sprites, icons, tilesets, portraits) for a game or UI-heavy build in a way that yields a coherent, intentional look instead of a debug-console aesthetic. Use this skill when you need art for rooms, entities, UI controls, HP bars, spell icons, status effects, or any other visual element; when the build is failing its UI-quality gate because it looks unintentional; or when you're about to fall back to raw ASCII/text UI. The goal is "looks like someone designed it" — not photorealism, not 1:1 with AAA games, but internally consistent and readable.

Ship a change to the GAD framework itself (new skill, new subagent, reworked command, altered loop) in a way that preserves the ability to interpret eval results across versions. Trigger this skill whenever a framework change could affect how an agent behaves during an eval run — e.g. adding a mandatory skill, changing a command's spawned subagent, rewording AGENTS.md, changing the plan-phase flow, anything that would make "the same agent prompt" produce different output. The goal is to make framework drift visible so we don't mistake a framework improvement for an agent improvement (or vice versa) when scores move.
Generate tests for a completed phase based on UAT criteria and implementation
Capture idea or task as todo from current conversation context
Audit milestone completion against original intent before archiving
Cross-phase audit of all outstanding UAT and verification items
Run all remaining phases autonomously — discuss→plan→execute per phase
List pending todos and select one to work on
Archive completed milestone and prepare for next version
Systematic debugging with persistent state across context resets

Gather phase context through adaptive questioning before planning. Use --auto to skip interactive questions (Claude picks recommended defaults). Use --chain for interactive discuss followed by automatic plan+execute.
Route freeform text to the right GAD command automatically
Compile planning docs from all roots into docs_sink as MDX files
Generate or update project documentation verified against the codebase
Execute all plans in a phase with wave-based parallelization
Post-mortem investigation for failed GAD workflows — analyzes git history, artifacts, and state to diagnose what went wrong
Diagnose planning directory health and optionally repair issues
Surface Claude's assumptions about a phase approach before planning
Interactive command center for managing multiple phases from one terminal
Fiction writing adaptation of the GAD loop for novels and story outlines
Analyze codebase with parallel mapper agents to produce .planning/codebase/ documents

Manage the full milestone lifecycle — start a new milestone (new version, new cycle), audit a completed one for gaps before archiving, and close it out with git tag and state reset. Use this skill when the user says "we're starting v2", "let's kick off the next release", "we finished everything in this milestone, what's next", "archive this milestone", or wants to review whether all planned work is actually done before closing. Also use it when all phases in the roadmap are marked done and you need to decide whether to start fresh planning or close out. Covers gad:new-milestone + gad:complete-milestone + gad:audit-milestone.
Generate a comprehensive project summary from milestone artifacts for team onboarding and review
Start a new milestone cycle — update PROJECT.md and route to requirements
Initialize a new project with deep context gathering and PROJECT.md
Automatically advance to the next logical step in the GAD workflow
Zero-friction idea capture. Append, list, or promote notes to todos.
Create phases to close all gaps identified by milestone audit
Create detailed phase plan (PLAN.md) with verification loop
Capture a forward-looking idea with trigger conditions — surfaces automatically at the right milestone
Check project progress, show context, and route to next action (execute or plan)
Reapply local modifications after a GAD update
Research how to implement a phase (standalone - usually use /gad:plan-phase instead)
Analyze any codebase (local or GitHub URL) and extract implementation-agnostic requirements plus system-skill contracts for clean-room reimplementation
Request cross-AI peer review of phase plans from external AI CLIs
Review and promote backlog items to active milestone
Generate a session report with token usage estimates, work summary, and outcomes
Configure GAD workflow toggles and model profile

Verify and optimize gad snapshot output to fit within the sprint context window. Use after modifying snapshot logic, adding new planning file types, or when snapshot token count exceeds the target budget.
Verify planning doc updates before proceeding to the next task. Called between tasks during execute-phase to enforce per-task tracking. Checks TASK-REGISTRY.xml and STATE.xml are current.
Manage persistent context threads for cross-session work
Update GAD to latest version with changelog display
Retroactively audit and fill Nyquist validation gaps for a completed phase

Verify a completed phase achieved its goals. Runs build checks, validates deliverables exist, checks planning doc state, produces VERIFICATION.md. Works automated (eval agents) or interactive (human review). Use after completing a phase, before marking it done in ROADMAP.xml.
Validate built features through conversational UAT
Add a path as a planning root in gad-config.toml
Show all registered planning roots and their current status
Crawl monorepo for .planning/ directories and sync gad-config.toml roots
Produce a feature documentation MDX file into the docs sink. Use when a project feature needs clear human-readable documentation that persists across sessions.
Capture user intent for a project into planning docs. Use when starting a new project, clarifying what a project is for, or when requirements are missing or vague.
Produce a technical breakdown doc — architecture, data flow, component design. Use when a system needs to be explained structurally for agent or developer onboarding.

Keep the public-facing GAD site and generated publishing artifacts in sync with the framework as it evolves. Trigger this skill whenever an eval run finishes, a new finding is written, a skill or agent changes, requirements or decisions move, or planning/docs outputs need regeneration.
Score a round of GAD development work on this monorepo — framework overhead ratio, loop compliance, planning doc freshness, real-world brownfield metrics.

Analyze GAD trace data (.gad-log JSONL + .trace-events.jsonl + preserved eval traces) to produce usage reports such as tool mix, skill invocations, commit rhythm, token coverage, runtime mix, and per-project breakdowns.
Use plans/summaries from INIT JSON, or list files
Check if STATE.md has quick tasks table
Get all commits ahead of target
Count outstanding items in current phase
